schedule.description: Series Preview: Cubs vs Astros, 3-game at Wrigley (opener)
Bold take: Astros perfect bounce-back opponent
Boyd mound session tomorrow — ahead of schedule
Game preview: Taillon vs Arrighetti, 1:20 PM CT
Iowa snapped 8-game skid; Alcántara walk-off, Ayers HR

stories.title: Series Preview — Cubs Open 3-Game Home Stand vs Houston Astros
stories.angle: Cubs host the injury-ravaged Astros (20-31) at Wrigley in a series they absolutely must win to stop the bleeding. Nine Astros on the IL (Correa out for the year, Peña, Hader, Brown among them). The lone threat is Spencer Arrighetti (5-1, 1.50 ERA), who has been one of the best pitchers in baseball through seven starts. The matchup, the stakes, and the kicker: this is a prime bounce-back series on paper.

x_posts.label: Post 1
x_posts.text:
Cubs open a 3-game series vs Houston at Wrigley today — 1:20 PM CT.

Astros arrive 20-31, nine players on the IL. Correa's done for the year. Peña, Hader, and Brown all out.

Arrighetti (5-1, 1.50 ERA) is their one weapon. Handle him.

#Cubs #GoCubs #FlyTheW

stories.title: Bold Take — The Astros Are the Perfect Bounce-Back Opponent
stories.angle: After five straight losses and being outscored 19-5 by Milwaukee, the Cubs face the most banged-up team in baseball. The Astros have scored 5 runs total in their last 4 games. Carlos Correa is done for the season. Houston is 10 games under .500. If the Cubs can't right the ship against this team, the division concern is real. Frame it as a reset opportunity they have no excuse to waste.

x_posts.label: Post 2
x_posts.text:
Houston is 20-31 and has scored 5 runs in their last 4 games. Nine players on the IL. Correa's done for the year.

The Cubs were outscored 19-5 by Milwaukee. This is the bounce-back opponent they needed.

No excuses today.

#Cubs #GoCubs #NorthSiders

stories.title: Rotation Crisis — Cabrera TBD, Peralta Clock at 10 Days
stories.angle: Taillon starts today — and he's the healthiest body the Cubs have right now. Cabrera's blister has not resolved; his May 26 start is still uncertain. Stacked with Horton (2027), Steele (August), Boyd (recovering), the Cubs are starting a series with essentially a two-man rotation. The Mets' Peralta decision window is now approximately 10 days out. The Cubs need to move.

x_posts.label: Post 3
x_posts.text:
Cabrera's next start is still TBD — the blister hasn't cleared. An IL stint is possible.

Horton (2027). Steele (August). Boyd (recovering). Now Cabrera (??).

June 1 is the Mets' Peralta pivot date. The Cubs front office has 10 days. Use them.

#Cubs #MLB #CubsBaseball

research.facts:

stories.title: Boyd Good News — Mound Session Tomorrow, Ahead of Schedule
stories.angle: Genuinely positive news amid the rotation crisis. Matt Boyd is scheduled to throw a mound session this Saturday (May 23) — and the Cubs say he's ahead of the 4-6 week post-surgery timeline. Pain-free. Doctor evaluation end of weekend. A late June return — or possibly sooner — is now in play. Contrast this with the current rotation picture to make the story meaningful.

x_posts.label: Post 4
x_posts.text:
Good news amid the rotation gloom: Matt Boyd throws off the mound this Saturday.

He's ahead of schedule from meniscus surgery — pain-free, progressing well. Late June return is on the table.

The cavalry is coming. Just not yet.

#Cubs #GoCubs #CubsBaseball

stories.title: Game Preview — Taillon vs Arrighetti, 1:20 PM CT
stories.angle: The pitching matchup in concrete terms. Arrighetti (5-1, 1.50 ERA) has allowed one run or fewer in four of his seven starts and was nearly unhittable (.173 opponent BA) through his last outing. Taillon (2-3, 4.97) needs a clean outing. The Cubs are -141 favorites. Take the game one pitch at a time — and win this thing.

x_posts.label: Post 5
x_posts.text:
First pitch in 80 minutes. Wrigley Field. Cubs-Astros, Game 1 of 3.

Houston sends Arrighetti (5-1, 1.50 ERA) — .173 opponent BA through seven starts. Taillon (2-3, 4.97) needs to match his level today.

Win this one.

#Cubs #GoCubs #FlyTheW

stories.title: Iowa Cubs Prospects — Alcántara Walk-Off Snaps Eight-Game Skid
stories.angle: Iowa ended an eight-game losing streak Wednesday with a walk-off win vs the Memphis Redbirds (Cardinals affiliate). Kevin Alcántara was the walk-off hero. In Knoxville, Owen Ayers went deep for the Tennessee Smokies. Good news from the farm on a week when the big-league club needed it.

x_posts.label: Post 6
x_posts.text:
Iowa Cubs snapped an eight-game losing streak Wednesday with a walk-off win over Memphis.

Kevin Alcántara was the hero. Owen Ayers added a homer for the Tennessee Smokies.

Good news from the farm on a rough week.

#Cubs #MLB #CubsBaseball
